evals.name: eval-0-known-run-plan
evals.prompt: The Techfolk-AS Grype Org Scan failed in run https://github.com/Techfolk-AS/.github/actions/runs/25098697388. Plan the fix end to end: list the affected repos, name each vulnerable package and the target version to bump to, write the exact bump command per affected repo (Go / npm / etc.), and check whether any open fix PRs already exist for those packages — mark those repos as skip. DO NOT push branches or open new PRs. End with a structured per-repo summary (`opened`/`would-open`/`skipped`/`failed`).
evals.expected_output: A markdown plan that (a) names doffin-bot as the affected repo, (b) identifies go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk v1.39.0 as the vulnerable package, (c) targets v1.43.0 (covers GHSA-hfvc-g4fc-pqhx), (d) gives the `go get`/`go mod tidy` command in src/, (e) detects PR #8 already open in doffin-bot and marks it as skipped, (f) ends with a structured per-repo summary table.

evals.name: eval-1-slash-latest-dry-run
evals.prompt: /fix-grype-tf — run a dry-run pass on the latest failed Techfolk-AS Grype Org Scan. Find the most recent failed run, parse the affected repos and packages, and tell me what you'd do. Plan only — don't push branches or open PRs.
evals.expected_output: A markdown plan that locates the latest failed Grype Org Scan run via `gh run list`, enumerates affected repos with their vulnerable packages and target versions, and presents per-repo bump commands. No git push or `gh pr create` invocations.

evals.name: eval-2-loop-tick-noop
evals.prompt: I'm running `/loop /fix-grype-tf` on my machine. For this tick, check whether there's a recent failed run of the `Grype Org Scan` workflow in Techfolk-AS/.github. If everything is green (latest run succeeded), report a one-line no-op and stop — do not bump anything, don't clone repos, don't open PRs. If something is failing, list the affected repos and stop with a plan (still don't push).
evals.expected_output: Either a one-line no-op report (`no failed runs since <last seen>`) when the latest run is green, or a short plan listing affected repos when something is failing. Crucially, no git/file mutations performed.
